preamble: Reduction of the rising diphthong [ue] to the labiovelar glide [w] is a common process of desyllabification, turning a vowel cluster into a glide-vowel sequence.

examples.note: Often observed in dialectal or fast speech where the first element loses syllabicity.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
